# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.207.0/http/server.ts";

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  city: string;
  age: number;
}

// Define fields that can be used for search filters and will have secondary indices.
const INDEXABLE_FIELDS: (keyof User)[] = ["name", "email", "city", "age"];

// Helper function to preprocess field values for consistent indexing (e.g., lowercase for strings).
function getIndexableFieldValue(field: keyof User, value: string | number): string {
  if (typeof value === 'string') {
    return value.toLowerCase();
  }
  return String(value); // Convert numbers or other types to string for KV key consistency.
}

async function handleRequest(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const pathParts = url.pathname.split("/").filter(Boolean); // e.g., ["users", "123"]

  if (pathParts[0] === "users") {
    const userId = pathParts[1]; // Can be undefined for /users (list/search endpoint)

    switch (request.method) {
      case "POST":
        return createUser(request);
      case "GET":
        if (userId) {
          return getUserById(userId);
        } else {
          // If no userId is specified, it's a list or search operation.
          return searchUsers(url.searchParams);
        }
      case "PUT":
        if (userId) {
          return updateUser(userId, request);
        }
        break; // Fall through to 404 if PUT /users without ID
      case "DELETE":
        if (userId) {
          return deleteUser(userId);
        }
        break; // Fall through to 404 if DELETE /users without ID
    }
  }

  return new Response("Not Found", { status: 404 });
}

// Retrieves a user's full data from the primary KV key.
async function getUserData(userId: string): Promise<User | null> {
  const userEntry = await kv.get<User>(["users", userId]);
  return userEntry.value;
}

// Sets user data in the primary store and updates all associated secondary indices.
// Secondary index keys are constructed dynamically based on INDEXABLE_FIELDS.
async function setUserDataWithIndices(user: User): Promise<Deno.KvCommitResult> {
  const op = kv.atomic();
  op.set(["users", user.id], user); // Primary storage: ['users', userId] -> User object

  for (const field of INDEXABLE_FIELDS) {
    const fieldValue = user[field];
    if (fieldValue !== undefined) {
      const indexValue = getIndexableFieldValue(field, fieldValue);
      // Secondary index: ['users_by_field', fieldName, processedFieldValue, userId] -> true
      op.set(["users_by_field", field, indexValue, user.id], true);
    }
  }

  return op.commit();
}

// Deletes user data from the primary store and all associated secondary indices.
async function deleteUserDataWithIndices(userId: string, existingUser: User): Promise<Deno.KvCommitResult> {
  const op = kv.atomic();
  op.delete(["users", userId]); // Delete primary storage

  for (const field of INDEXABLE_FIELDS) {
    const fieldValue = existingUser[field];
    if (fieldValue !== undefined) {
      const indexValue = getIndexableFieldValue(field, fieldValue);
      op.delete(["users_by_field", field, indexValue, userId]);
    }
  }

  return op.commit();
}

async function createUser(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const user: Partial<User> = await request.json();
    if (!user.id || !user.name || !user.email || !user.city || user.age === undefined) {
      return new Response("Missing required fields (id, name, email, city, age)", { status: 400 });
    }

    const existingUser = await kv.get(["users", user.id]);
    if (existingUser.value) {
      return new Response("User with this ID already exists", { status: 409 });
    }

    const newUser: User = {
      id: user.id,
      name: user.name,
      email: user.email,
      city: user.city,
      age: user.age,
    };

    const commitResult = await setUserDataWithIndices(newUser);
    if (!commitResult.ok) {
      return new Response("Failed to create user", { status: 500 });
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(newUser), {
      status: 201,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error creating user:", error);
    return new Response("Internal Server Error", { status: 500 });
  }
}

async function getUserById(userId: string): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const user = await getUserData(userId);
    if (!user) {
      return new Response("User not found", { status: 404 });
    }
    return new Response(JSON.stringify(user), {
      status: 200,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error getting user:", error);
    return new Response("Internal Server Error", { status: 500 });
  }
}

async function updateUser(userId: string, request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const updates: Partial<User> = await request.json();

    const existingUserEntry = await kv.get<User>(["users", userId]);
    if (!existingUserEntry.value) {
      return new Response("User not found", { status: 404 });
    }

    const existingUser = existingUserEntry.value;
    const updatedUser: User = { ...existingUser, ...updates, id: userId }; // Ensure ID remains immutable

    const op = kv.atomic();
    op.set(["users", userId], updatedUser); // Update primary record

    // Update indices for changed fields.
    for (const field of INDEXABLE_FIELDS) {
      const oldFieldValue = existingUser[field];
      const newFieldValue = updatedUser[field];

      const oldIndexValue = oldFieldValue !== undefined ? getIndexableFieldValue(field, oldFieldValue) : undefined;
      const newIndexValue = newFieldValue !== undefined ? getIndexableFieldValue(field, newFieldValue) : undefined;

      if (oldIndexValue !== newIndexValue) {
        // If the field's value (or its indexable representation) has changed.
        if (oldIndexValue !== undefined) {
          op.delete(["users_by_field", field, oldIndexValue, userId]); // Delete old index entry.
        }
        if (newIndexValue !== undefined) {
          op.set(["users_by_field", field, newIndexValue, userId], true); // Add new index entry.
        }
      }
    }

    const commitResult = await op.commit();
    if (!commitResult.ok) {
      return new Response("Failed to update user", { status: 500 });
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(updatedUser), {
      status: 200,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error updating user:", error);
    return new Response("Internal Server Error", { status: 500 });
  }
}

async function deleteUser(userId: string): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const existingUserEntry = await kv.get<User>(["users", userId]);
    if (!existingUserEntry.value) {
      return new Response("User not found", { status: 404 });
    }

    const commitResult = await deleteUserDataWithIndices(userId, existingUserEntry.value);
    if (!commitResult.ok) {
      return new Response("Failed to delete user", { status: 500 });
    }

    return new Response(null, { status: 204 });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error deleting user:", error);
    return new Response("Internal Server Error", { status: 500 });
  }
}

async function searchUsers(queryParams: URLSearchParams): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const filterConditions: { field: keyof User; indexValue: string }[] = [];
    for (const [key, value] of queryParams.entries()) {
      if (INDEXABLE_FIELDS.includes(key as keyof User)) {
        // Construct search condition using the preprocessed indexable value.
        filterConditions.push({ field: key as keyof User, indexValue: getIndexableFieldValue(key as keyof User, value) });
      }
      // Non-indexable query parameters are ignored for filtering, but could be used for pagination/sorting.
    }

    let userIdsIntersection: Set<string> | null = null;

    if (filterConditions.length === 0) {
      // If no search parameters, list all users (requires iterating the primary 'users' prefix).
      userIdsIntersection = new Set<string>();
      const entries = kv.list<User>({ prefix: ["users"] });
      for await (const entry of entries) {
        if (entry.value) {
          userIdsIntersection.add(entry.value.id);
        }
      }
    } else {
      // Apply each filter condition using secondary indices and intersect the results.
      for (const condition of filterConditions) {
        const currentBatchIds = new Set<string>();
        // Construct prefix for the secondary index: ['users_by_field', fieldName, processedFieldValue]
        const prefix = ["users_by_field", condition.field, condition.indexValue];

        const entries = kv.list({ prefix: prefix });
        for await (const entry of entries) {
          // The last part of the index key is the userId.
          const userId = entry.key[entry.key.length - 1] as string;
          currentBatchIds.add(userId);
        }

        if (userIdsIntersection === null) {
          userIdsIntersection = currentBatchIds; // This is the first filter condition applied.
        } else {
          // Intersect with results from previous filters (AND logic).
          userIdsIntersection = new Set([...userIdsIntersection].filter(id => currentBatchIds.has(id)));
        }

        if (userIdsIntersection.size === 0) {
          // Early exit if the intersection results in no matching users.
          break;
        }
      }
    }

    const results: User[] = [];
    if (userIdsIntersection && userIdsIntersection.size > 0) {
      // Fetch the full user objects for all IDs that matched the search criteria.
      const promises = [...userIdsIntersection].map(id => getUserData(id));
      const fetchedUsers = await Promise.all(promises);
      for (const user of fetchedUsers) {
        if (user) {
          results.push(user);
        }
      }
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(results), {
      status: 200,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error searching users:", error);
    return new Response("Internal Server Error", { status: 500 });
  }
}

// Start the Deno HTTP server.
serve(handleRequest);
